commentary: Excited to share that the mRNA-4157 Phase 2 interim analysis is underway. Fascinating methods challenge: handling interval-censored endpoints with time-varying covariates. The biostatistics team at Windbridge Partners is doing incredible work.

Just wrapped up another semester teaching PH 206 Introduction to Biostatistics for Public Health at Amberfield Institute. Incredibly rewarding to see students connect statistical theory to real public health problems. Always learning as much as I teach.
Great read on mixed-effects models for longitudinal clinical trial data. The discussion of dropout mechanisms is especially relevant for our current work. Link in comments.
